level_signals: 5+ years of experience in operations, technology, banking, analytics or related experience

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 5+ years of experience in operations, technology, banking, analytics or related experience with a track record of leading initiatives and hitting goals
You have strong cross functional business experience, project management, and communication skills

You are comfortable working with large data sets + modeling in Excel / Google Sheets, proficiency in SQL, and well-versed in data visualization tools (e.g. Sigma, Tableau) to help solve ambiguous problems
You are willing to roll up their sleeves and flex up or down as needed - "humble doer"
You have a Bachelor's degree or higher

scope.surface: Post-Sales SMB Account Management — sales strategy and operations supporting SMB restaurant partners
scope.business_model: B2B marketplace (DoorDash merchant partnerships with SMB restaurants, grocers, convenience stores, retailers)
core_responsibilities: Partner with post-sales SMB team to accelerate growth for SMB restaurants
Drive operational excellence in reporting cadences, pipeline tracking, and sales productivity
Lead end-to-end ideation to execution through data-driven insights
Partner with cross-functional teams to expand and elevate DoorDash's offering for SMBs
Ensure GTM processes across products and initiatives are effective and efficient for sales teams
Shepherd Account Management team into the future leveraging AI tooling
Make efficient, data-driven decisions and optimize SMB Account Management operational processes

green_flags: Comprehensive benefits package described (401k, parental leave, wellness, medical/dental/vision)
Clear team surface and responsibilities described
Applications accepted on an ongoing basis — not time-pressured
red_flags: Work location type is not specified in the JD body (remote, hybrid, or onsite unclear)
No cities listed for this role specifically — location pay differential mentioned but no city-level ranges provided
No mention of manages_people but title is 'Associate Manager' — could create expectation mismatch

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Associate Manager' and comp range)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false; no direct reports mentioned in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and merchant partner context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
location.work_auth (inferred as open; no restrictions stated)
ambiguities: Work location type (remote/hybrid/onsite) is never specified in the JD body. The national pay range mentions Illinois and Colorado, and the NYC AEDT notice is included, suggesting the role may be open in multiple locations or remote — but this is not confirmed.

The 'Associate Manager' title typically implies people management in some organizations, but the JD describes no direct reports or people management responsibilities. Treated as individual contributor here.

'Bachelor's degree or higher' is phrased as a requirement under 'We're excited about you because…' which could be read as a soft signal, but the phrasing is unconditional, so classified as hard severity.
